rulings.Case Title: ASMIR FERHATOVIC VS. EUROPEAN HARDWOOD FLOORS INC.AKA EHF INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MARCH 11, 2008 LINE 3. DEFENDANT EUROPEAN HARDWOOD FLOORS INC. A.K.A. E.H.F. INC., ALEXANDER MEDVEDEV AND LUDMILA MEDVEDEV'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. The Court's analysis is based on the ruling in Burnside v. Kiewet Pacific Corp. (9th Cir. 2007) 491 F.3d 1053. If the claim is derived solely from the Collective Bargaining Agreement ("CBA"), it is preempted by federal law. If there is a state law right, the Court then determines whether the state law right stands on its own without considering the CBA. If the answer is that there is such an independent state law claim, the Court is required to determine whether the state law claim is substantailly dependent on the analysis of the CBA, that is whether the state law claim may be resolved by looking to as contrasted with interpreting the CBA. If the claim requires the Court to interpret the CBA, the claim is preempted. The demurrer is sustained to the 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, and 6th causes of action. Plaintiff's claims are based on the CBA. As to the 4th cause of action, a claim for wrongful discharge based on union activity is preempted by NLRA sections 7 and 8 and that is what plaintiff appears to allege in paragraphs 19 and 29 of the complaint. As to the 6th cause of action, the separate state law basis in Labor Code section 310 is not viable given the language of Labor Code section 514 and the existence of the CBA sections 22, 24, 26, 31 and 39. It does not appear that plaintiff has a basis to amend but plaintiff may present argument on this point if plaintiff desires to seek leave to amend. The demurrer to the 5th, 7th, 8th, 9th and 10th causes of action is overruled; 10 days to answer. There is an independent basis under California law for each of these causes of action and the Court is not required to interpret the CBA to decide these claims. =(302/PJM/AY)
